name: Zerodha-Style Stock Portfolio REST API
scenario: Build a complete REST API for a stock portfolio management system. Users can manage their stock positions: add holdings, view portfolio, update quantities, remove positions, and get summary statistics. Full CRUD with correct HTTP status codes, request/response DTOs, and @WebMvcTest coverage.
description: A production-style Spring Boot REST API with @RestController, all HTTP methods, ResponseEntity, request/response records, and @WebMvcTest tests. No database yet â€” in-memory storage via a service layer.

core.description: Request and response records: `PositionRequest(String symbol, int shares, double avgBuyPrice)` and `Position(String id, String symbol, int shares, double avgBuyPrice, double currentPrice, double unrealizedPnL)`.

`PortfolioService` (@Service): in-memory Map storage. Methods: findAll(), findById(String), addPosition(PositionRequest), updateShares(String id, int shares) â†’ Optional, removePosition(String id) â†’ boolean, getSummary() returning PortfolioSummary(totalValue, totalPnL, positionCount).

`PortfolioController` (@RestController at /api/portfolio): GET all positions (200), GET /positions/{id} (200 or 404), POST /positions (201), PUT /positions/{id}/shares (200 or 404), DELETE /positions/{id} (204 or 404), GET /summary (200).
All correct HTTP status codes: 200 GET, 201 POST, 204 DELETE, 404 not found. All 404 cases return empty body.
GET /api/portfolio/positions with optional query params: `?symbol=TCS` (filter by symbol) and `?profitable=true` (only positions with positive PnL).
@WebMvcTest suite: minimum 5 tests â€” GET all, POST 201, GET by ID found, GET by ID 404, DELETE 204. Use @MockBean PortfolioService.

bonus.description: Add PUT /api/portfolio/positions/{id} for full position update (all fields). Test with MockMvc: found returns 200 with updated data, not-found returns 404.
Add GET /api/portfolio/positions/search?q={text} searching across symbol names. Return list of matching positions. Verify with jsonPath in @WebMvcTest.
Add PATCH /api/portfolio/positions/{id}/price to update currentPrice for a position (simulating a price feed update). Return 200 with recalculated PnL.

data_to_use:
Sample portfolio:
TCS: 10 shares, avgBuy=â‚ą2760, current=â‚ą3890 â†’ PnL=+â‚ą11300 (+40.9%)
RELIANCE: 20 shares, avgBuy=â‚ą2450, current=â‚ą2450 â†’ PnL=â‚ą0 (0%)
INFY: 30 shares, avgBuy=â‚ą1600, current=â‚ą1560 â†’ PnL=-â‚ą1200 (-2.5%)

Summary: totalValue=â‚ą159700, totalPnL=+â‚ą10100, positions=3

curl examples:
POST /api/portfolio/positions
  body: {"symbol":"TCS","shares":10,"avgBuyPrice":2760}
  â†’ 201 + {"id":"POS-001","symbol":"TCS",...}

GET /api/portfolio/positions/POS-001 â†’ 200 + position
GET /api/portfolio/positions/POS-999 â†’ 404
DELETE /api/portfolio/positions/POS-001 â†’ 204

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key patterns: GET by ID â†’ Optional.map(ResponseEntity::ok).orElse(notFound). DELETE â†’ boolean ? noContent : notFound. @WebMvcTest: @MockBean for service, MockMvc.perform(get/post/put/delete). Content type header on POST: .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON). jsonPath assertions: jsonPath('$.id').value('POS-001').
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. Include a README with curl commands demonstrating all endpoints.
tips_for_success: PortfolioSummary: sum all (shares Ă— currentPrice) for totalValue
GET all with filters: @RequestParam(required=false) String symbol, Boolean profitable
@WebMvcTest: one test per HTTP scenario, not per endpoint
